Michael Lenczner
Home Effective Hallucinations

Michael Lenczner has spent two decades at the intersection of technology and social change — founding initiatives in the community wireless and open data movements, and working across governments, funders, and nonprofits. He is the founder and CEO of DARO, a consulting firm that helps mission-driven organizations navigate change and have impact. He is also a School Fellow at Carleton University's School of Public Policy and Administration.

LinkedIn Bookmarks

Writing

  • June 10, 2026

    Blog Post 1: Beyond Data Maturity

    Why the nonprofit sector should retire 'data maturity' and adopt 'adaptive capacity' as its organizing frame for data and learning.

  • June 10, 2026

    Blog Post 3: No Solution, Only Honest Mitigation

    The structural problems facing nonprofit accountability don't have clean solutions. The honest move is to stop pretending they do — and work carefully within the constraint.

  • June 10, 2026

    Blog Post 2: Where Adaptive Capacity Comes From

    Two structural problems — the principal-agent problem and resource dependence — explain why nonprofits struggle to build adaptive capacity, and why data practices alone can't fix it.
